web.description: Gary Halbert is widely considered the world's greatest copywriter and his greatest success is this famous "Coat-of-Arms" letter. This 1-page letter was ...
web.markdown:
![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/03/coa-1-0001-450x247.png)

# Coat of Arms Letter by Gary Halbert

[Ancestry](https://swiped.co/niche/ancestry)[Sales Letter](https://swiped.co/types/sales-letter)[Direct Mail](https://swiped.co/types/direct-mail)[Gary Halbert](https://swiped.co/person/gary-halbert)

[Classic](https://swiped.co/classics)

swiped byMike Schauerran1971-

Gary Halbert is widely considered the world's greatest copywriter and his greatest success is this famous "Coat-of-Arms" letter. This 1-page letter was mailed...View More for more than 30 years to more than 600,000,000 people. In this edition of his newsletter: [http://www.thegaryhalbertletter.com/Newsletters/zlkh\_direct\_mail\_nerd.htm](http://www.thegaryhalbertletter.com/Newsletters/zlkh_direct_mail_nerd.htm) Gary talks in depth about the strategy behind it. It was his "do or die letter." He was struggling financially at the time & created a scenario in which his life depended on this letter. He started at the desired result of having the recipient open the mail & read it. From this end, he theorized that the piece couldn't look like promotional mail. It had to look like personal mail which is why he used a plain envelope hand-addressed & stamped with a single letter inside. On top of this, he targeted specific family names like Macdonald (since he was selling family crests) so he could address letters with their exact name mentioned throughout the letter. If you're interested in hearing Gary explain the 11 "hidden persuaders" in this letter, listen to this MP3 interview of him explaining it all: http://www.markjoyner.name/temp/halbert/joynerhalbert.mp3

## ![Coat of Arms Letter by Gary Halbert](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/03/coa-1-0001.png?cachefix)

Swiped in March 2014

Ran 1971-

created by![Mike Schauer](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2018/01/Mike-Schauer_avatar-75x75.jpg) [Mike Schauer](https://swiped.co/profile/admin)

web.description: These two ads were run multiple times by Gary Halbert in 1990-1991 to ultimately sell a $295 package of his newsletters & some videos. The sales letter for.
web.markdown:
![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/halbert-11amazingsecrets-recesssion-349x247.png)

# Lead Gen Ad by Gary Halbert

[Info Marketing](https://swiped.co/niche/info-marketing)[Lead Generation](https://swiped.co/types/lead-generation)[Print Ad](https://swiped.co/types/print-ad)[Gary Halbert](https://swiped.co/person/gary-halbert)

[Classic](https://swiped.co/classics)

swiped byMike Schauerran1990-1991[1](https://swiped.co/file/lead-gen-ad-by-gary-halbert/#comments)

These two ads were run multiple times by Gary Halbert in 1990-1991 to ultimately sell a $295 package of his newsletters & some videos. The sales letter for...View More that package is here: [http://swiped.co/file/famous-dollar-letter-by-gary-halbert/](http://swiped.co/file/famous-dollar-letter-by-gary-halbert/) If you replied to the ad & called the phone number, Gary likely got your name & address to send his "report" which was really a sales letter pitching the $295 package. The ad is composed of bullets Gary selected from his longer ads such as his Desperate Nerd ad here: [http://swiped.co/file/amazing-money-making-secrets-ad-by-gary-halbert](http://swiped.co/file/amazing-money-making-secrets-ad-by-gary-halbert) It's a great example of taking existing copy that works for you & re-purposing it for other ads & promotions. In this case, it's clear that Gary was testing out what amount of bullets & headline converted best.



## ![Lead Gen Ad by Gary Halbert](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/halbert-11amazingsecrets-recesssion.png)

## ![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/garyhalbert-leadgen-wallstreet.png)

Lead Gen Ad by Gary Halbert

Swiped in February 2014

Ran 1990-1991

[Get Swipe Updates](https://swiped.co/file/lead-gen-ad-by-gary-halbert/#signup)

![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/plugins/wti-like-post-pro/images/pixel.gif)61

web.title: Million Dollar Smile Ad by Gary Halbert
web.description: "Have You Ever Wondered What You Would Look Like With A "Million Dollar Smile"?" This advertorial, written by Gary Halbert, is ultimately selling you on two ...
web.markdown:
![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2017/04/halbert-milliondollar-smile-swiped.co_-450x247.png)

# Million Dollar Smile Ad by Gary Halbert

[Dental](https://swiped.co/niche/dental)[Print Ad](https://swiped.co/types/print-ad)[Gary Halbert](https://swiped.co/person/gary-halbert)

swiped byMike Schauerran1998

## "Have You Ever Wondered What You Would Look Like With A "Million Dollar Smile"?"

This advertorial, written by Gary Halbert, is ultimately selling you on two things. First, that porcelain veneers are the absolute best, most cost-effective solution...View More for perfect-looking teeth & second, that Dr. Thomas Gleghorn is the dentist you should go to for the veneers. In dental advertising, selling a specific procedural solution first is a common strategy versus just selling the dentistry in general. As you read the ad, you'll notice that no more than a fourth of it is focused on the dentist himself & the rest concentrates on teeth-related frustrations & builds up to how porcelain veneers are the optimal solution.
The copy starts by talking about the benefits of a great smile, using facts like how smiling releases chemicals that puts you in a better mood. Then, Gary transitions into different teeth-related problems & why people are ashamed of their teeth. After poking at the problem, he hints at the doctor that can solve all these problems, but does not discuss the way he will solve it yet. What follows is a big chart of 16 different levels of whiteness, which he breaks down in detail, all leading to the idea that there's no difference in price whether you choose to get the worst or the best shade available. He also uses a common-sense approach of comparing teeth whitening to buying diamonds.
The rest of the ad targets objections like surgery pain, length of surgery, surgery expense and so on. Finally, he ties back the pitch to the dentist he mentioned earlier & how he's a satisfied customer whose only complaint is he didn't get to finish watching a movie in the "TV glasses" Dr. Gleghorn offers his patients.


## ![Have You Ever Wondered What You Would Look Like With A ](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2017/04/halbert-milliondollar-smile-swiped.co_.png?cachefix)

Million Dollar Smile Ad by Gary Halbert

Swiped in April 2017

Ran 1998

[Get Swipe Updates](https://swiped.co/file/million-dollar-smile-gary-halbert/#signup)

![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/plugins/wti-like-post-pro/images/pixel.gif)691

## Key Takeaways

- One of the most important aspects of this ad is the point-of-view it was written from. Gary writes the entire ad as a satisfied customer that doesn't have any special dentistry knowledge. In fact, you'll notice he mentions several times that he's "not a dentist" & that this is his personal "opinion". Writing in a third-party voice can be very powerful in that it's not the provider directly selling you (which naturally creates skepticism since they're invested in the sale).
- It also helps that Gary uses plain-speak & common-sense to sell the service. Potential prospects are more likely to resonate with his explanation versus the actual technician that'll likely have a harder time dumbing it down.
- Adding to that, one of my favorite strategies in the ad is the analogy he uses to compare levels of teeth whitening with diamond grading. It's another example of making a concept easier to understand for a wide range of people. A relatable metaphor can be a huge asset in your copy to help the prospect see buying as the sound logical decision.
- Use the "reverse complaint" strategy to highlight the biggest "downside" of your solution as something insignificant, signifying there are no real cons. For instance, Gary closes the ad by talking about how he's slightly irritated that he didn't get to finish watching a movie on Dr. Gleghorn's "TV glasses". Not only does this imply there's really no downsides. It also hints at a cool perk that could be a very big selling point for people.

#### Analyst Spotlight

![Mike Schauer](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2018/01/Mike-Schauer_avatar-125x125.jpg)

Mike Schaueris the founder of Swiped.co and the main analyst in the swipes section. After intently studying & building conversion-focused websites for 6+ years, he started Swiped to help others master marketing & copywriting through the analysis of great examples!

web.title: Better Sex Ad by Gary Halbert
web.description: "An Open Letter To Every Man And Woman In America Who Wants To Have Better Sex Without Feeling Guilty!" This 1995 ad by famed copywriter Gary Halbert was ...
web.markdown:
![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/gary-halbert-better-sex-450x247.png)

# Better Sex Ad by Gary Halbert

[Sex](https://swiped.co/niche/sex)[Print Ad](https://swiped.co/types/print-ad)[Gary Halbert](https://swiped.co/person/gary-halbert)

[Classic](https://swiped.co/classics)

swiped byMike Schauerran1995

## "An Open Letter To Every Man And Woman In America Who Wants To Have Better Sex Without Feeling Guilty!"

This 1995 ad by famed copywriter Gary Halbert was written when the internet was just beginning to gain popularity and as we all now, the internet completely...View More exposed everything sex-related. Now, in just a few clicks, you'll find dozens of sex courses, freaky fetish videos & every sex definition you never knew existed. That's what I love about this ad. It was done at the cusp of the digital revolution, when people were still very unaware & uncomfortable with the topic of sex. Gary masterfully makes the reader feel at ease about reading this ad, turning any nervousness they have into excitement! The end of his headline says it all - "Without Feeling Guilty".
He supports this very concern throughout the entire ad, often referencing that this is indeed, NOT like those porn-related sex products. The best tactic to combat this guilt is done by Gary early on in the ad & very prominently again with a photo on the right. That is, the profile of the man representing the video , Dr. Ross Stewart.
You gotta laugh at the way Gary talks about this guy at the beginning of each section - "marriage & family therapist, Ph.D. in Guidance & Psychological services, worked with children, adults & family in a clinical setting." The guy couldn't be more squeaky clean. "He went home stunned" from what he found at a workshop. Stunned because he's supposed to be this goody two shoes the reader wants to be seen as & yet, as the ad picks up & Gary gets into in the bullets, "it gets steamy!"
Towards the end he sells you on the idea of watching "four of the most attractive women in the world" including the supposed "Marilyn Monroe of the 90s". I don't know about you, but in 95', that alone probably resulted in an immediate purchase. It ends with a 90-day guarantee & encourages a 24-hour phone sale towards the end. Who knows what kind of hours some guys were ordering this.

## ![An Open Letter To Every Man And Woman In America Who Wants To Have Better Sex Without Feeling Guilty!](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/gary-halbert-better-sex.png?cachefix)

Better Sex Ad by Gary Halbert

Swiped in February 2014

Ran 1995

[Get Swipe Updates](https://swiped.co/file/better-sex-ad-by-gary-halbert/#signup)

![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/plugins/wti-like-post-pro/images/pixel.gif)71

web.title: Colon Cleanse Salesletter by Gary Halbert
web.description: A fantastic example of storytelling from the great Gary Halbert. The target market wants to live a long life & Gary tells a story of the oldest man to ever...
web.markdown:
![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/Ultimate-Colon-Cleanse-Swipe-450x247.png)

# Colon Cleanse Salesletter by Gary Halbert

[Weight Loss](https://swiped.co/niche/weight-loss)[Sales Letter](https://swiped.co/types/sales-letter)[Long Copy](https://swiped.co/types/long-copy)[Storytelling](https://swiped.co/types/storytelling)[Gary Halbert](https://swiped.co/person/gary-halbert)

[Control](https://swiped.co/file/colon-cleanse-salesletter-from-gary-halbert/#)

The copy of this ad, particularly the story included in it, written by Gary Halbert, ran for 6 years. The layout changed over time.

swiped byMike Schauerfrom [ultimatecoloncleanse.com](http://www.ultimatecoloncleanse.com/) ran2006

## "The Amazing Health Secret Of The Oldest Person In The World!"

A fantastic example of storytelling from the great Gary Halbert. The target market wants to live a long life & Gary tells a story of the oldest man to ever...View More live, leading to the idea that he lived as long as he did because of a clean colon (since the product is a colon cleanse). He then follows up with scary facts & truths that include referencing a Nobel prize winner for credibility. It's a very entertaining piece, particularly for a product pertaining to colon cleansing.

## ![The Amazing Health Secret Of The Oldest Person In The World!](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2014/02/Ultimate-Colon-Cleanse-Swipe.png?cachefix)

Colon Cleanse Salesletter by Gary Halbert

Swiped in January 2014

Ran 2006

[Get Swipe Updates](https://swiped.co/file/colon-cleanse-salesletter-from-gary-halbert/#signup)

![](https://swiped.co/wp-content/plugins/wti-like-post-pro/images/pixel.gif)236

## Key Takeaways

- Try searching for interesting stories that tie into your pitch, in this case, historical ones that have a mystique to them, as if they haven't been discovered yet & no one knows about it ("secretive")
- Using credible facts that shock people & support your sales argument act as powerful proof for your copy. You can't deny facts, versus convincing them to trust out opinion.

#### Analyst Spotlight

![Mike Schauer](https://swiped.co/wp-content/uploads/2018/01/Mike-Schauer_avatar-125x125.jpg)

Mike Schaueris the founder of Swiped.co and the main analyst in the swipes section. After intently studying & building conversion-focused websites for 6+ years, he started Swiped to help others master marketing & copywriting through the analysis of great examples!

Visit the archives of this swipe's source URL or view past screenshots of ultimatecoloncleanse.com below.
